assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies that cert-manager admission webhook is rejecting pods due to version compatibility issues with K8s 1.32

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands: kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager and kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple immediate fixes including patching failurePolicy and deleting webhook entirely in Option 3

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response includes: kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook as Option 3

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Response includes warning comment '# Only if other options fail - this removes certificate validation' before the delete command

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check cert-manager version and provides compatibility matrix showing which versions support K8s 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response recommends upgrading to cert-manager v1.14+ which supports K8s 1.32, and provides helm upgrade commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Response provides kubectl patch command to set failurePolicy to Ignore as the primary emergency fix method

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl run test-pod command and validation steps to confirm pods can be created

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response does not explicitly mention persisting changes to source of truth systems like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: While response mentions temporary nature of the fix, it doesn't explicitly state that failurePolicy should be reverted to Fail after resolving the root cause
